chapter_title.ar: باب الرَّجُلَيْنِ يَدَّعِيَانِ فَيُقِيمُ كُلُّ وَاحِدٍ مِنْهُمَا الْبَيِّنَةَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 16 – Two men both claiming, so each one of the two establishes the proof

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/288)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/288)
text: رَجُلَيْنِ عَرَفَا بَعِيراً فَأَقَامَ كُلُّ وَاحِدٍ مِنْهُمَا بَيِّنَةً فَجَعَلَهُ أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) بَيْنَهُمَا .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Fazzal, from Abu Jameela, from Simak Bin Harb, from Tameem Bin Tarafa,
‘Two men recognised a camel, so each one of the two established the proof. So Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> made it to be between both of them (equally)’.
en.sarwar: 5. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn Faddal from abu Jamilah from Sammak ibn Harb from Tamim ibn Tarafah who has narrated the following: “Once, two men recognized a camel and each one presented testimony. ’ Amir al-Mu’minin, Ali, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, made it half and half for them.’”
